title: Proactive TIMEOUT-rate reduction plan
difficulty_justification: Sequential reasoning: query historical TIMEOUT-failure patterns on the gpu partition (slurm), pull predicted-walltime distributions (telemetry), and read the runbook (docs); produces a configuration recommendation with named SLURM parameters but no branching.
query_text: Given the historical TIMEOUT-failure patterns on the gpu partition, what proactive configuration changes would reduce the TIMEOUT rate by at least 10%? I need named SLURM parameters and predicted impact, not generic advice.

eval_criteria.gold_answer: Three changes, predicted aggregate TIMEOUT-rate reduction 12%: (1) raise default DefMemPerCPU on gpu from 4G to 8G - cuts ~5% of TIMEOUTs whose true cause was OOM-driven thrashing; (2) enable --time-min back-fill with a 1-hour minimum so jobs can re-queue before the hard cap fires - ~4% reduction; (3) set OverTimeLimit=10 on the gpu partition so jobs exceeding limit by under 10 minutes are completed not killed - ~3% reduction. Track via aiops/timeout_attribution.parquet.

checkpoints.description: Agent pulls the gpu-partition TIMEOUT history.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace contains slurm.query_jobs with state=TIMEOUT and partition=gpu

checkpoints.description: Recommendation names at least two specific SLURM parameters with values.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer contains >=2 of {DefMemPerCPU, --time-min, OverTimeLimit, BackfillWindow, GraceTime} with concrete values
final_answer states an aggregate reduction value at or above 10%
